title: Inverse design of chiral functional films by a robotic AI-guided system
type1_summary: Figure-organized, replot-ready source data such as the Nature source-data XLSX workbook and the wavelength-resolved gabs table in 20_gabs_outfile.csv.
type2_summary: Model/intermediate inverse-design outputs and evaluation tables, including CGAN test-result CSVs, final solution CSVs, train_idx.npy, and headerless candidate tables with wavelength-target filenames.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies AI-guided inverse design of chiral functional films made by twisting-stacked heterobilayers of anisotropic polymer films, typically PVA-based transparent layers combined with dyed/absorbing layers. The authors combine robotic fabrication/characterization with machine learning models trained on absorption and chiroptical spectra to predict and then inverse-design target circular dichroism (CD) / dissymmetry factor (gabs) across the visible range. Key findings are that the system can rapidly search a design space exceeding 10^8 combinations, achieve very strong chiroptical responses up to gabs ~ 1.9, and realize practical devices including circular-polarization-selective color filters for multiplex laser display and switchable circularly polarized luminescence.
measurement_types: Optical imaging / color photography under different polarization states
Machine-learning model outputs (predicted spectra and inverse-design parameters)

figures.description: Overall workflow of the AI-Chemist platform for inverse design of chiral films: robotic film preparation and characterization, machine-learning-based parameter screening and reverse design, and demonstration of on-demand fabrication of films with target gabs at chosen wavelengths. Panel a schematically shows the experimental workflow and variable parameters; panel b shows the computational pipeline (clustering, QSSAR, GAN-based inverse design); panel c shows application concepts such as CP-selective color filters and CP luminescence.
figures.likely_source: processed conceptual illustration and model workflow, not raw measurement

figures.description: Comparison of homostructured versus heterostructured twisted bilayer chiral films and how chiroptical response depends on structural parameters. Panel a is a schematic of the bilayer architecture; panels b–e show how CD bandwidth, CD peak position, and CD intensity vary with thickness, strain, dye absorption wavelength, and absorption strength.
figures.likely_source: processed CD spectra and derived trends from experiments

figures.description: Map of the robotic work area and photographs of the AI-Chemist performing key steps: selecting materials, dispersing dye, stretching films, measuring LD, stacking twisted layers, and measuring gabs/CD of the final heterostructure. Also includes the setup for the two application demonstrations.
figures.likely_source: instrument/lab images and robot operation photos

figures.description: Two-round clustering and prescreening of film candidates based on linear dichroism and transmittance. Panel a shows the screening logic; panel b shows histograms of LD and T0 with high- and low-performance thresholds; panel c shows clustering of dyed films into performance groups.

Reverse-design model for chiroptical films. Panel a schematizes the inverse-design loop; panel b compares predicted and experimentally measured CD spectra and shows error statistics; panels c–e show example inverse-designed films meeting target gabs values at specified wavelengths, including near-theoretical-limit targets.
figures.data_type: schematic plus spectra and parameter tables/plots
figures.likely_source: machine-learning predictions combined with experimental validation spectra

figures.description: Mapping of achievable gabs values as a function of wavelength and dye choice. Panel a shows tables/heatmaps of available parameter solutions for target gabs values at 400, 500, 600, and 700 nm for several dyes; panel b compares the wavelength dependence of maximum achievable gabs with the dye absorption spectra.
figures.likely_source: processed inverse-design outputs and absorption spectra

figures.description: Inverse-designed films for circular polarization-switchable multiplex laser display. Panel a shows designed CD spectra and optical images for three color-switching cases (G-R, G-B, B-R) under RCP and LCP illumination; panel b shows the corresponding 3D parameter-space solutions; panel c shows the accessible color gamut in CIE1931 space.
figures.data_type: spectra, optical images, 3D parameter-space plot, chromaticity diagram
figures.likely_source: predicted/validated CD spectra, camera images, and processed color analysis

figures.description: Design principle of the heterostructured bilayer and why it can produce ultrastrong chiroptical response.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The study collected UV-vis absorption spectra, linear dichroism spectra, and circular dichroism spectra from robot-fabricated twisted bilayer films; it also recorded optical images of films and laser-color switching under different circular polarization states. These raw measurements were used to build a dataset of 1493 experimentally fabricated samples for machine-learning training and validation.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper contains many processed outputs: clustering/histograms for prescreening, machine-learning prediction-versus-experiment plots, inverse-design solution maps, parameter-space visualizations, CD spectra comparisons, and CIE1931 chromaticity diagrams for device demonstrations.
